chapter_title.ar: بَابُ مَنْ أَكَلَ أَوْ شَرِبَ نَاسِياً فِي شَهْرِ رَمَضَانَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 21 – The one who eats or drinks out of forgetfulness during the Month of Ramazan

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (16/271)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (16/271)
text: هُ سُئِلَ عَنْ رَجُلٍ نَسِيَ فَأَكَلَ وَ شَرِبَ ثُمَّ ذَكَرَ قَالَ لَا يُفْطِرْ إِنَّمَا هُوَ شَيْ‏ءٌ رَزَقَهُ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ فَلْيُتِمَّ صَوْمَهُ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, and Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, altogether from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Hammad Bin Usman, from Al Halby,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having been asked about a man who forgot, so he ate and drank, then remembered. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘He should not break the Fast. But rather, it is something Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic Graced him, so let him complete his Fast’.
en.sarwar: 1. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from and Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad all from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Hammad ibn ‘Uthaman from al-Halabiy who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘ Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who because of forgetfulness eats and drinks and then remembers. He (the Imam) said, ‘His fast is not destroyed. It was something Allah, most Majestic, most Glorious, granted him. He is required to complete his fast (of that day).”’
